basics.name: Arafat Khan
basics.summary: An AI engineer who does a little bit of everything — backend, infra, agents, and the occasional love letter.

work.name: Cline
highlights: Building the world's best AI Coding Agent

work.name: Sourcegraph
highlights: Built an enterprise AI coding assistant

work.name: ImmigrantFirst
highlights: Built a state of the Art LLM framework to build O1 Visa applications for US immigration using Generative AI.

work.name: Shopify
highlights: Co-implemented a load-balancer-level split of read and write requests using Kubernetes for Shopify's financial transaction application, improving p99 request times across most of Shopify's payment transactions by 50%.
Enhanced networking and infra pipeline to make our payments infrastructure fully compliant with PCI/DSS regulations and pass the audit.
Built, maintained, and upgraded a highly scalable, fault-tolerant MySQL database for critical credit card data.

work.name: Shopify
highlights: Designed a major-tech technical pipeline touching multiple Rails services to find major bottlenecks in Shopify's fraud assessment flow and improved checkout latency by 60%.
Managed autoscaling for Black Friday sales as the traffic spiked to 10x the normal limit.

work.name: Simon Fraser University
highlights: Applied ML techniques on CT scans of body segments for fat analysis and body composition labelling for cancer patients.

awards.title: Google Summer of Code
awards.summary: Built the best plotting library for Ruby to support visualisations for science experimentation in Ruby — https://github.com/SciRuby/rubyplot

awards.title: Ruby Grant
awards.summary: Official grant from Ruby Association to build TensorFlow in Ruby — https://github.com/somaticio/tensorflow.rb

skills.name: Public Speaking
keywords: DevopsDays Recife: Digital nomading in tech
Gophercon India: Algo visualizations in Go
Ruby on Ice, Germany: ML workshop

projects.name: Tensorflow.rb
highlights: Spoke about this project at many tech conferences across Europe and North America.
Project was eventually endorsed by the Ruby Association.
Project trended on GitHub for several days.

projects.name: Dataviz
projects.summary: An algorithms explanation library written in Go.
highlights: Designed a user-friendly codebase to understand complex algorithms visually.
Presented this work at Gophercon in Belarus and India.

projects.name: Glot
projects.summary: A plotting library in Go built on top of Gnuplot.
highlights: Added Go support for styles like lines, points, bars, steps, histograms, and circles.
Presented this work at Gophercon in Belarus and India.
